technical_terms_dropped: avyaya rendered 'unchanging' loses the ṣaḍ-vikāra-denial sense
avināśi rendered 'indestructible' is faithful but loses the 'beyond destruction-as-category' Shankara reading
verdict_reason: This verse is the second place in chapter 2 where Shankara and Ramanuja sharply disagree on scope (universal Brahman pervading the universe vs. localised jīva pervading its own body). The engineering layer correctly does not adjudicate. The analog ('protocol pervades implementation') is shaped narrowly enough to apply to either reading: a contract pervades many implementations (universal-flavor) or a single jīva pervades a single body (localised-flavor). The disagreements field surfaces the divergence honestly. The engineering layer does not silently adopt one tradition's reading. The terms preserved verbatim (avināśi, avyaya, tatam) are presented with glosses, not with sectarian interpretations. PASS.
